text: As I observe these scenes, I feel a mingling of curiosity and detachment. The first image places me outside a glass-walled meeting or classroom, glimpsing people immersed in discussion or work, and I sense a subtle tension between inclusion and exclusion—am I an observer, or do I long to participate? In the second image, I stand before an exhibit about research and development, its bright colors and bold lettering evoking energy and innovation. Here, I feel inspired by the possibilities of creation, yet also reflective: so much of human progress is displayed, narrated, and mediated by institutions. I am prompted to ponder the nature of knowledge, participation, and the ways we gather in spaces designed for thinking and making.
